content:
# CudaGrader Buffer Pre-Allocation Design (2026-04-03)

Issue: chunzhe10/dorea#27
Design doc: `docs/decisions/2026-04-03-cuda-grader-buffer-pre-allocation.md`
Plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-03-cuda-grader-buffer-pre-allocation.md`

## Decision
Pre-allocate all device buffers in `CudaGrader` — two complementary constructors:

### 1. `CudaGrader::new()` — dynamic (default)
Two `RefCell<Option<...>>` cached buffer structs keyed by shape:
- `ResolutionBuffers` — keyed by (width, height). 8 slices.
- `CalibrationBuffers` — keyed by (n_zones, lut_size). 6 slices.
Reuse via `htod_sync_copy_into` (no alloc). Reallocate only on shape change.
Zero `cudaMalloc` per frame in steady state. Handles 1080p/4K transparently.

### 2. `CudaGrader::with_capacity(max_w, max_h)` — static
Pre-allocates `ResolutionBuffers` at construction. Zero `cudaMalloc` on every frame including the first. Frames exceeding `(max_w, max_h)` return `GpuError::InvalidInput`.
`grade_frames_with_capacity()` batch wrapper in `lib.rs`.

## Key safety notes
- Input length guards before `borrow_mut` blocks — `htod_sync_copy_into` panics (not errors) on length mismatch inside a live `RefMut`, poisoning the `RefCell`
- `n_zones == 0` returns `GpuError::InvalidInput` early
- `// SAFETY:` comment at buffer invalidation: `dtoh_sync_copy` calls `device.synchronize()` at frame end, guaranteeing stream idle before next buffer realloc
- `per_frame_vram_bytes` must use 4 RGB planes (not 3) — Task 5 in plan

## VRAM held per grader
- 1080p: ~89 MB
- 4K: ~347 MB (~6% of 6 GB budget)

## Files changed
- `crates/dorea-gpu/src/cuda/mod.rs` — only file for core change
- `crates/dorea-gpu/src/lib.rs` — `grade_frames_with_capacity` wrapper
